key_insight: HPG-Diff combines physics-guided diffusion with a differentiable connectivity penalty to generate topology designs that better follow load paths and avoid floating material.
engineering_relevance: The method improves topology optimization generalization under unseen boundary conditions while producing more mechanically plausible, connected structures.
method: Hierarchical physics guidance injects precomputed physics features into the denoising trajectory.
A floating-material suppression loss uses virtual heat propagation from load points as a differentiable connectivity constraint.
Lightweight LoRA fine-tuning adapts the model to rectangular non-square domains with limited data.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports average compliance errors of 0.87% in-distribution and 5.29% out-of-distribution, with floating material ratios reduced to 2.90% and 2.44%, respectively.
watch_item: The approach depends on precomputed physics features and training-time connectivity modeling, so performance may be sensitive to feature quality and adaptation settings in new geometries.
paper_title: HPG-Diff: Hierarchical physics-guided diffusion with differentiable connectivity constraints for topology optimization
